qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Zone-file $INCLUDE on live reload leaks service-readable files to low-privilege local writers
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns unrestricted `$INCLUDE` processing on zone-file live reload paths in `plugin/file` and `plugin/auto`, not the advisory's missing early size validation on the DoH GET `dns=` path in `plugin/pkg/doh/doh.go`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default proxyproto trust lets network clients bypass IP-based ACL decisions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns default trust of PROXY protocol headers and downstream ACL/view authorization decisions, not the advisory's DoH GET oversized `dns=` parsing and pre-validation resource-amplification path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a DoH GET denial-of-service issue caused by missing early size validation before query parsing, base64 decoding, and DNS unpacking on `/dns-query?dns=...`; the qualifying findings instead target unrelated `$INCLUDE` reload disclosure and `proxyproto` trust behavior.
evidence: The advisory root cause is specific to `plugin/pkg/doh/doh.go` GET handling (`requestToMsgGet()` passing the oversized `dns` value into `base64ToMsg()`) and missing early request-size validation on the HTTPS DoH path.

The `file_include_secret_leak` finding names `plugin/file/file.go`, `plugin/file/reload.go`, and `plugin/auto/walk.go` and requires a low-privilege local writer to inject `$INCLUDE` into zone reload input; it does not involve DoH, `/dns-query`, base64 decoding, or pre-validation resource amplification.

The `proxyproto_default_acl_bypass` finding names `plugin/proxyproto/setup.go`, `request/request.go`, and ACL/view consumers and depends on forged PROXY headers influencing client-IP authorization, which is a different component, sink, and security failure mode from the advisory.
